body: import org.apache.commons.codec.EncoderException;
import org.junit.Test;
import org.apache.commons.codec.StringEncoderAbstractTest;
import org.junit.Assert;

translation: # treat vowels and HW as separators
assert s.encode1("Dwdds") == "D320" # w is a separator
assert s.encode1("Dhdds") == "D320" # h is a separator
# treat vowels and HW as silent
assert s.encode1("Dodds") == "D200" # 'o' is not a separator here - it is silent

body:
public void testHWRuleEx3() throws EncoderException {
        Assert.assertEquals("S460", this.getStringEncoder().encode1("Sgler"));
        Assert.assertEquals("S460", this.getStringEncoder().encode1("Swhgler"));
        this.checkEncodingVariations(
                "S460",
                new String[] {
                    "SAILOR",
                    "SALYER",
                    "SAYLOR",
                    "SCHALLER",
                    "SCHELLER",
                    "SCHILLER",
                    "SCHOOLER",
                    "SCHULER",
                    "SCHUYLER",
                    "SEILER",
                    "SEYLER",
                    "SHOLAR",
                    "SHULER",
                    "SILAR",
                    "SILER",
                    "SILLER"
                });
    }

public void testEncodeIgnoreHyphens() throws EncoderException {
        this.checkEncodingVariations(
                "K525",
                new String[] {
                    "KINGSMITH",
                    "-KINGSMITH",
                    "K-INGSMITH",
                    "KI-NGSMITH",
                    "KIN-GSMITH",
                    "KING-SMITH",
                    "KINGS-MITH",
                    "KINGSM-ITH",
                    "KINGSMI-TH",
                    "KINGSMIT-H",
                    "KINGSMITH-"
                });
    }
